note: M12a (F5) — BSP front-to-back traversal oracle (R_RenderBSPNode), first sub-step of M12. reference_model.bsp_render_order(cmap, vx, vy) = near-first DFS (the viewer's side, via shared _point_side) -> subsectors nearest-first (the wall draw order for solid-seg clipping); visible_segs = segs flattened in that order. Iterative (explicit stack) -- the M7-built BSP is deep/unbalanced. Tested on the concave L-shape: permutation, viewer-subsector-first, near-subtree-before-far, segs-follow-order.
invariants: order is a permutation of all subsectors
root near-subtree fully drawn before far-subtree
known_blocker: Building the full E1M1 BSP overflows the M7 RECURSIVE builder (mapcompiler.compile_bsp -> build_bsp -> recurse, ~1829 segs). M10 deferred BSP-as-code for this. NEXT STEP M12b: make compile_bsp iterative so E1M1 builds, enabling traversal + the renderer on E1M1.
